MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
CaptureByTimer C++

CaptureByTimer illuminates how to capture videos when being triggered by preset timer. That is, capture videos at the specified time, and the frame rate is ajustable.
Compared with CaptureByInput C++, this example is used when a flexible frame rate is required.
Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\Legacy_VC++\CmdTools\CaptureByTimer

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family

Features

  1. Capture the video data at the specified frame rate.
  2. Save the last frame as a .bmp file.
  3. Select the capture channel from multiple devices by specifying the command value.

Flow Chart

CaptureByTimer

Workflow

  1. To get device version, initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWGetVersion, MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount;
  2. To find the valid capture card according to the device info,, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex;

  3. Conduct any of the following steps to open a channel:

    1. To open the specified channel according to the command parameter, call: MWOpenChannel;
    2. To open the first available channel by default when there is not a specified channel, call: MWGetDevicePath, MWOpenChannelByPath;
  4. To start video capture, call: MWStartVideoCapture;
  5. To register a timer, call: MWRegisterTimer;
  6. To set the trigger time, and capture a frame when receive the trigger, call: MWScheduleTimer, MWCaptureVideoFrameToVirtualAddressEx;
  7. To get current capture status, and clear the memory of capture card, call: MWGetVideoCaptureStatus;
  8. Save the last frame as a BMP file.
  9. To stop recording, and free up system resources and memory, call: MWUnregisterTimer, MWStopVideoCapture, MWCloseChannel, MWCaptureExitInstance

Result

CaptureByTimer
